skill.name: art-style-creator
skill.description: Create practical art style guides for games, illustrations, animation, UI, and other visual projects.
skill.summary: Build structured art direction documents with color, line, shape, composition, lighting, texture, mood, and technical rules.
skill.author: CesarAugustusGroB

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found them to be false positives in Markdown documentation. The flagged backticks are fenced examples and diagrams, while the weak-crypto and reconnaissance alerts match art terms such as shadow, mask, contrast, black, and color references. No evidence found of command execution, network access, credential access, filesystem modification, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Static Analysis False Positives in Markdown Reference Material
low_findings.description: The reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns occur inside Markdown diagrams, tables, and art style templates. They are not executable code and do not request sensitive data or system access.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed locations are Markdown code fences, art tables, or glossary entries, not runnable scripts. A targeted search found no prompt-injection language, network calls, environment access, or command execution APIs.

content.value_statement: Visual projects often drift when teams lack clear style rules. This skill turns art direction choices into a structured guide for colors, shapes, lighting, texture, mood, and delivery specs.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through project vision, audience, influences, and style keywords.
Defines color palettes with usage ratios, mood alignment, and forbidden colors.
Documents line work, shape language, composition, lighting, texture, and mood rules.
Adds technical specifications for resolution, color mode, file formats, and asset sizes.
Provides quick genre presets for anime, cartoon, realistic, pixel art, noir, fantasy, sci-fi, horror, and minimalist styles.
Produces a complete Markdown art style guide template for team use.
limitations: It does not generate finished artwork or image assets by itself.
It depends on user-provided project context, references, and creative goals.
It gives general art direction and cannot replace legal clearance for copyrighted reference material.
It does not validate color accessibility, production budgets, or platform constraints automatically.

use_cases.title: Define a Game Art Direction
use_cases.description: Create shared visual rules for characters, environments, UI assets, lighting, and export specifications before production starts.
use_cases.target_user: Indie game developers and art directors

use_cases.title: Align Illustration Commissions
use_cases.description: Turn a creative brief into clear palette, line, mood, and composition guidance for artists and reviewers.

use_cases.title: Standardize Animation Style
use_cases.description: Document shape language, cel shading choices, background detail, and emotional tone for consistent scenes.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Basic Style Guide
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an art style guide for a small fantasy game. Ask me only the key questions needed to define the visual direction.

prompt_templates.title: Build From References
prompt_templates.scenario: User has influences and needs structure
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me define a visual style using these influences: [list influences]. Separate what to borrow, what to avoid, and what should be original.

prompt_templates.title: Specify Production Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-ready style guide for [project]. Include palette ratios, line weights, shape rules, lighting rules, texture levels, and export specs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Style Consistency
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this art direction draft for consistency gaps: [paste draft]. Identify conflicts, missing rules, unclear constraints, and production risks.

output_examples.input: Create a style guide for a cozy farming game with soft fantasy elements.
output: A warm analogous palette with green, yellow, and soft coral accents.
Rounded shapes, low-contrast lighting, soft texture, and gentle detail concentration around characters.
Technical notes for UI icons, character sprites, background layers, and final export formats.

output_examples.input: Define a noir visual style for a detective comic.
output: A monochrome base with one restrained accent color for clues and emotional beats.
Heavy angular silhouettes, sharp line work, high contrast lighting, and rain or smoke texture cues.
Rules for forbidden bright colors, shadow treatment, panel composition, and reference use.

output_examples.input: Make our sci-fi UI assets feel consistent with the game world.
output: A cool RGB palette, clean geometric shapes, thin outlines, and controlled neon accents.
Asset size guidance for icons, panels, screen graphics, and export formats.
Do and do not rules for glow, contrast, shape repetition, and technical naming conventions.

best_practices: Provide project type, audience, tone, platform, and reference influences before requesting the guide.
Ask for measurable rules such as color ratios, line weights, asset sizes, and export formats.
Use the generated guide as a living document and revise it after real production tests.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a vague style without defining audience, mood, medium, or production constraints.
Do not copy a named artist or franchise directly when an original direction is required.
Do not treat the guide as final until sample assets prove the rules work together.

faq.question: Can this skill create finished artwork?
faq.answer: No. It creates structured art direction and style documentation for humans or image tools to follow.

faq.question: Does it work for games and animation?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow covers characters, environments, UI elements, lighting, mood, and technical asset specifications.

faq.question: Can it help with color palettes?
faq.answer: Yes. It defines palette type, dominant colors, secondary colors, accents, mood alignment, and colors to avoid.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code compatible workflows.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Reviewed static alerts were false positives in Markdown art documentation, with no executable behavior found.

faq.question: What should I prepare before using it?
faq.answer: Prepare the project type, target audience, emotional tone, visual references, platform, and production constraints.
